articles.title: Luka Dončić Pledges Financial Support to Adou Thiero After Game 4 Ejection
articles.original_title: Lakers’ Luka Doncic makes financial pledge to Adou Thiero after embarrassing Game 4 ejection - New York Post
articles.summary: After an embarrassing ejection in Game 4, Dallas Mavericks star Luka Dončić publicly promised to provide financial assistance to Rockets forward Adou Thiero. The pledge came amid criticism of the officiating that led to the ejection.
key_points: Dončić’s pledge was made in response to Thiero’s ejection during the Lakers‑Rockets playoff game.
The incident highlighted concerns over inconsistent refereeing in the series.
Both players received media attention for the off‑court gesture.

articles.title: LeBron James Says Lakers Feel Uncomfortable Despite 3‑1 Series Lead Over Rockets
articles.original_title: LeBron James: Lakers Aren’t Comfortable Despite 3-1 Series Lead On Rockets
articles.summary: LeBron James noted that the Los Angeles Lakers are not feeling at ease even though they hold a 3‑1 lead in the first‑round series against the Houston Rockets. The Lakers dropped Game 4, preventing a sweep, and will look to rebound in Game 5 at home.
key_points: Lakers lost Game 4, ending the chance for a sweep.
James emphasized lingering discomfort and the need to tighten play.
Series shifts back to Los Angeles for Game 5, where the Lakers aim to close it out.

articles.title: LeBron James and Luka Dončić Defend Adou Thiero After Controversial Game 4 Ejection
articles.original_title: LeBron James and Luka Dončić stood up for Adou Thiero after Game 4 ejection
articles.summary: Both LeBron James and Luka Dončić publicly supported Rockets forward Adou Thiero following a disputed ejection late in Game 4. The incident involved a minor scuffle with Aaron Holiday that many felt should not have resulted in an ejection.
key_points: James and Dončić voiced criticism of the refereeing decision.
The ejection occurred during garbage‑time moments of the game.
The controversy added tension to an already heated playoff series.

articles.title: Marcus Smart Finds Rockets’ Trash‑Talk ‘Hilarious’ After Game 4 Blowout
articles.original_title: Marcus Smart Thinks Trash-Talk From Jae’Sean Tate & Rockets Was ‘Hilarious’
articles.summary: Following the Rockets’ Game 4 victory that avoided a sweep, Marcus Smart commented on the trash‑talk from Rockets forward Jae’Sean Tate, calling it “hilarious.” The game featured multiple technical fouls, ejections, and heightened emotions between the two squads.
key_points: Smart laughed at Tate’s comments, indicating a light‑hearted response to the rivalry.
Game 4 was marked by several technical fouls and player ejections.
The series now returns to Los Angeles with the Lakers still holding a 3‑1 lead.

articles.title: Lakers’ Turnover Woes Cost Them Against Rockets
articles.original_title: The Lakers have a big turnover problem against the Rockets
articles.summary: In Game 4 of the first‑round series, the Los Angeles Lakers fell to the Houston Rockets, jeopardizing a potential sweep that hadn’t been achieved since 2010. The loss highlighted the Lakers’ turnover problem, especially as Kevin Durant remained out, allowing a young Rockets squad to play with urgency.
key_points: Lakers lost Game 4, ending their chance for a first‑round sweep.
Turnovers were a critical factor in the defeat.
Kevin Durant’s continued absence gave Houston an advantage.

articles.title: Sengun Defends Ayton After Ejection Controversy
articles.original_title: Alperen Sengun Doesn’t Think Deandre Ayton Should’ve Been Ejected
articles.summary: Alperen Şengün expressed that Deandre Ayton should not have been ejected during Game 4, noting that Ayton’s physical defense helped the Lakers build a 3‑0 series lead. Şengün praised Ayton’s ability to match his intensity and make life difficult for the Rockets’ All‑Star.
key_points: Şengün believes Ayton’s ejection was unwarranted.
Ayton’s defensive effort contributed to the Lakers’ 3‑0 lead.

articles.title: Rookie of the Year Finalists Named: Flagg, Knueppel, Edgecombe
articles.original_title: NBA set to give out Rookie of the Year award; Flagg, Knueppel and Edgecombe finalists
articles.summary: The NBA has announced the finalists for the Rookie of the Year award: Cooper Flagg (Dallas), Kon Knueppel (Charlotte), and Jaden Edgecombe (Seattle). Flagg and Knueppel, former Duke teammates, are projected as the top two candidates, with the winner to be revealed shortly after 7 p.m. ET.
key_points: Cooper Flagg (Dallas Mavericks) and Kon Knueppel (Charlotte Hornets) are the leading contenders.
Jaden Edgecombe (Seattle SuperSonics) completes the finalist trio.
The award announcement is scheduled for shortly after 7 p.m. Eastern Time.

articles.summary: Former Knicks guard Donte DiVincenzo suffered a devastating Achilles tear, becoming the fourth player in the past two playoffs to miss the remainder of the season with such an injury. Knicks point guard Jalen Brunson expressed his sympathy for DiVincenzo’s situation.
key_points: DiVincenzo’s Achilles rupture ends his season during the playoffs.
